one_time_description: Duck Lake project contributions ($2,275,000) and UXO removal costs ($2,000,000) appear to be non-recurring items in fiscal 2021
economic_notes: Band owns OKIB Group of Companies (OKIB Forestry LP, OKIB Operations LP, OKIB Assets LP, OKIB Management Services LP), Okanagan Indian Band Land Holding Company Ltd., and Okanagan Band Development Corporation. Also owns 631640 B.C. Ltd. (consolidated subsidiary). Band provided guarantees for 12 housing loans totalling $2,350,557 outstanding. Ottawa Trust funds held under Indian Act Sections 62-69.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to verify tangible capital assets acquired prior to 2010 — cost and acquisition dates not determinable. Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. General revenue detail from Note 21 includes First Nations Gaming Revenue Share ($794,213), First Nations and other funding ($1,193,174), CMHC subsidies ($237,985), Daycare fees and subsidies ($26,545). Total government transfers calculated as federal ($18,912,794) + provincial ($1,891,725) = $20,804,519; OSR = $29,674,398 - $20,804,519 = $8,869,879. Note: Forestry Consultation and Revenue Sharing Agreement ($1,855,067) treated as OSR as it is not a direct government transfer program. Deferred revenue increased significantly by $7,186,126 to $10,531,914, largely driven by ISC and other restricted funding received but not yet spent. Two large non-recurring expense items: Duck Lake project contributions $2,275,000 and UXO removal costs $2,000,000. No schedule of remuneration provided. Council expenses detailed in Note 20 totalling $839,277. num_gbes=3 representing OKIB Group of Companies, OIB Land Holding Company Ltd., and Okanagan Band Development Corporation; num_subsidiaries=1 being 631640 B.C. Ltd. current_liabilities estimated as accounts payable $3,460,174 + due to government $21,322 + security deposits $3,580 = $3,503,076 (employee benefit obligations and deferred revenue excluded as separate line items).
